data.title: Sub-agent prompt guidance: mandatory read_first gate before edits
data.description:
Add a mandatory read_first gate to sub-agent dispatch prompts: when a task ticket lists files, the sub-agent must read every listed file before making any edits.

## Reference: get-shit-done read_first

get-shit-done (47k stars) includes a read_first field on every task definition:

\`\`\`xml
<task type="auto">
  <files>src/auth/login.py</files>
  <read_first>src/auth/base.py, src/models/user.py, tests/auth/test_login.py</read_first>
  <action>...</action>
</task>
\`\`\`

The execution rule is explicit and non-negotiable: "Tasks with read_first fields require reading EVERY listed file BEFORE edits — mandatory, non-negotiable." This prevents the common failure mode where agents edit files they haven't read, introducing changes that conflict with existing patterns, duplicate existing utilities, or break existing contracts.

## Problem

DSO sub-agents dispatched by sprint sometimes edit files without reading the full context of related files first. This leads to:
- Changes that duplicate existing utility functions
- Patterns that conflict with established conventions in the file
- Broken imports or interface contracts
- Test files written without understanding the existing test patterns

## Implementation

### 1. Task Ticket File Lists as read_first
DSO tasks already have file impact sections listing source and test files. These should be treated as a mandatory read_first gate:
- Sub-agent dispatch prompts must include an explicit instruction: "Before making any changes, read every file listed in the task's file impact section. This is mandatory and non-negotiable."
- The sub-agent checkpoint protocol (CHECKPOINT 1/6 — context loaded) should verify that all listed files were read.

### 2. Implementation-Plan Task Generation
When implementation-plan generates tasks, the file impact section should be expanded to include:
- **files_modified** — files that will be changed (existing)
- **read_first** — files that must be read for context before any edits begin (new). This includes: parent classes, imported modules, interface definitions, existing test files for the module, and configuration files that affect behavior.

### 3. Sub-Agent Dispatch Prompt Template
Update the sub-agent dispatch prompt template used by sprint to include:

"MANDATORY: Before writing any code, you must read every file listed below. Do not skip any file. Do not assume you know the contents. Read them all first, then proceed.

Files to read before editing:
[read_first list from task ticket]"

### 4. Checkpoint Verification
CHECKPOINT 2/6 (patterns understood) should explicitly confirm: "Read all [N] files listed in read_first. Key patterns observed: [summary]." If a sub-agent skips this checkpoint, the orchestrator should flag it during post-batch verification.
